USA Rugby

Quick Facts
  • A citizen of the United States as well as Great Britain and Bulgaria, Jayne won cadet national titles in both Great Britain and Bulgaria in 2013, but ultimately chose to represent the United States on the international stage

  • A wrestler for the University of Chicago, Jayne competed internationally in judo while pursuing a degree in mathematics

  • After graduating from the University of Chicago, Jayne moved down from 100 kg to 90 kg in 2019 and qualified for his first Senior World Championship in 2021 after the COVID-19 shutdown in 2020

  • A bronze medalist at his first Senior Pan American Championships in 2022, Jayne is currently the highest ranked U.S. man in the world rankings, holding the No. 30 position at 90 kg

Olympic Experience
  • 1-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Paris 2024, 9th (90kg - Men)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2021 – 33th (90kg - Men)
  • Years of participation: 90kg - Men 2021
  • Top finish: 33th – 2021 (90kg - Men)

EXPERIENCE
2016 Women's Elite Team Member
- World Cup of Softball participant
  - Led the Elite Team with three home runs, collecting a .364 (4-for-11) batting average with five RBI and three runs scored

AT UCLA
2016 All-Pac-12 Second Team
2015 All-Pac-12 Freshman Team

GETTING TO KNOW JELENICKI
Parents: Cris and Dan Jelenicki
Dream job:To be an ESPN broadcaster
On what it means to represent the United States in softball: It is truly an honor to be able to wear USA across my chest and get to play with some of the most talented athletes in the game.
What she enjoys most about softball: I love that the game is always challenging you and testing your mental toughness. You can strikeout one at bat and come up with the game on the line the next at bat and get a hit. There is always a chance to get better and learn from your mistakes.

FUN FACTS
Favorite activities:Yoga, working out, hanging with friends, the beach
Favorite snack/meal:Pita Chips and Hummus
Best vacation she's ever had: Oklahoma City my freshman year to play in the WCWS!
My cellphone wallpaper is:A picture of my Mom and I
Best advice I've ever received:"Work hard!"

ONE LAST THING
Last gift I bought: Bought myself some Lululemon
Last time I was embarrassed: When my backpack broke in the middle of campus and my papers were flying everywhere
Last home cooked meal: Salmon, rice and veggies
Last guilty pleasure: Chocolate covered almonds
Last thing I misplaced: My wallet

Quick Facts

  • Son of Constance Bullock and Eric Brown. Has three brothers - Paul, Marcus and Jamar, and a sister, LaTarsha. His partner is Laura Klass. Has a daughter named Saraeya.

  • Born with congenital amputation

  • Was a successful track athlete in his early years and a top wrestler in high school

  • Attended Huntingtown High School and joined the wrestling team, competing against able-bodied athletes and finishing third in his weight class at the Maryland state tournament his senior year

  • Started playing wheelchair basketball in 1992 with team Air Capital in Washington D.C

  • Was a three-year captain and two-time All-American for the Edinboro men’s wheelchair basketball team and played professional basketball for two years overseas in France

  • Penned an inspirational autobiography, “From the Ground Up”, at 17 years old

  • Favorite sports teams are the Washington Wizards and Washington Commanders

  • Aspires to work for the federal government and ultimately become a lawyer

  • Hobbies include hunting, fishing, shooting, camping and spending time with family and friends

Paralympic Experience
  • 4-time Paralympian; 4-time Paralympic medalist (3 gold, 1 bronze)
    • Paralympic Games Paris 2024, gold (Team - Men)
    • Paralympic Games Tokyo 2020, gold (Team - Men)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, gold (Team - Men)
    • Paralympic Games London 2012, bronze (Team - Men)

  • Years of participation: Team - Men 2014, 2018, 2023
  • Medals: 3 (1 gold, 2 silver)
    • Gold – 2023 (Team - Men)
    • Silver – 2018 (Team - Men); 2014 (Team - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Jennifer and Joseph Jenkins
  • Has one younger brother, Nathan
  • Born with left Proximal Femoral Focal Deficiency and underwent a left Symes Amputation when she was eight months old
  • Was fitted with her first prosthesis as a one-year-old and walked independently at 17 months
  • Learned to swim at age four and began competitive swimming when she was eight
  • Has two dogs, Indy and Lucy, and two cats, Moon and Ninja
  • Loves to travel and learn about different cultures
  • Considers herself a big TV and movie buff

    • Silver – 2019 (4 x 100m Freestyle Relay 34 Points - Women, 4 x 100m Medley Relay 34 Points - Women)
    • Bronze – 2022 (100m Butterfly S10 - Women)

bio.education: Brandywine High School (Wilmington, Del.) West Chester University ’13, B.A. Political Science, University of Delaware ’15, M.A. Political Science & International Relations, PhD ’18, Political Science & International Relations
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Son of Gordon and Elaine Jenks
  • Born with incomplete achromatopsia, a non-progressive and hereditary congential retinal condition characterized by decreased vision, light sensitivity and partial color blindness
  • Introduced to goalball in 2000 at a sports day sponsored by Delaware Association of Blind Athletes (DABA) at the Bob Carpenter Center at the University of Delaware
  • Met John Mulhern, a member of the U.S. Men’s Goalball Team that had just returned from the 2000 Paralympic Games in Sydney, and Greg Gontaryk – two coaches he credits with teaching him the ‘in’s and out’s’ at a young age
  • Hobbies include reading, exploring major metropolitan areas, going on brewery tours and tasting pretty much anything out there
  • Plays the saxophone and loves listening to and playing funk/jam music
  • Lives by the motto: “The world is your oyster so shuck it, put some hot sauce on it, and shoot it.”
Career Highlights
  • 2015 Parapan American Games, silver
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian; 1-time Paralympic medalist (1 silver)
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, silver (Team - Men)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2022 – 9th (Team - Men)
  • Years of participation: Team - Men 2014, 2022
  • Medals: 1 (1 bronze)
    • Bronze – 2014 (Team - Men)

bio.education: Herron High School (Indianapolis, Indiana) '11  Indiana State University '15, Political Science
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Son of Chris Jent and Sena Jessen
  • Has an older brother, Ryan
  • Nicknamed Ayden “the Jet” Jent after winning a national title in the 100m at his first competition in 2015
  • Born three months prematurely and diagnosed with Cerebral Palsy
  • Served as the team manager for the Indiana State University track and field and cross country teams for four years, helping the teams to win 11 conference championships
  • Served as a reporter and photographer for the ISU newspaper, the Indiana Statesman
  • Graduated with a degree in Political Science and a minor in Civic Leadership
  • His step-father, Jacques Jessen, helped him to discover his passion for running in 2007
  • Discovered Paralympics after watching a video of an athlete with the same impairment, inspiring him to contact the U.S. Paralympics Track and Field high performance director
  • Dreams of becoming a political campaign manager at the local level and working his way up to running for office
  • Sport idols include NASCAR driver Dale Earnhardt Jr, NFL player Adam Vinatieri, MLB player Jackie Robinson and U.S. Olympic sprinter Jesse Owens
  • Hobbies include running reading, photography, kayaking, dancing and watching movies.
Paralympic Experience
  • 1-time Paralympian
    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 5th (100m T35 - Men, 200m T35 - Men)
World Championship Experience
  • Most recent: 2017 – 5th (200m T35 - Men), 7th (100m T35 - Men)
  • Years of participation: 100m T35 - Men 2015, 2017; 200m T35 - Men 2015, 2017
  • Top finish: 5th – 2017 (200m T35 - Men)

Career Highlights
2017 Yucatan ITU Triathlon World Cup silver medalist
2017 Bridgetown CAMTRI Sprint Triathlon American Cup champion
2014 Geneva ETU Triathlon European Cup champion

Elite Triathlon Career

2017: Placed eighth at the Huelva ITU Triathlon World Cup on Sept. 23 • Earned her first career World Cup medal with a silver in Merida, Mexico (Yucatan), on Aug. 12 • Placed 15th at ITU World Triathlon Montreal on Aug. 5 • Started her season strong with a win at the Bridgetown CAMTRI Sprint Triathlon American Cup on April 23

2016: Took 14th at ITU World Triathlon Edmonton on Sept. 3 • Placed fifth at the Montreal ITU Triathlon World Cup on Aug. 7 • Recorded a 32nd-place finish at ITU World Triathlon Hamburg on July 16 • Took 14th at the Tiszaujvaros ITU Triathlon World Cup on July 9 • Placed 29th at ITU World Triathlon Yokohama on May 14 • Logged a 16th-place finish at ITU World Triathlon Cape Town on April 23 • Finished 23rd at ITU World Triathlon Gold Coast on April 9 • Opened her season with a 27th place finish at ITU World Triathlon Abu Dhabi

2015: Finished her 2015 ITU season with a seventh place finish at the Cozumel ITU World Cup • Posted 33rd place finishes at ITU World Triathlon Edmonton and the ITU World Triathlon Series Grand Final in Chicago • 26th place at the Rio de Janeiro ITU World Olympic Qualification Event, which served as the test event for the Rio 2016 Olympic Games • 18th at ITU World Triathlon Cape Town • Broke the top 10 at two ITU World Triathlon Series events in the early part of the 2015 season, finishing 8th at ITU World Triathlon Gold Coast and 6th in her season opener at ITU World Triathlon Abu Dhabi

2014: Placed 13th at the Cozumel ITU World Cup in October • Posted a career-best fifth-place finish at ITU World Triathlon Stockholm on Aug. 23 • Finished fifth at the Tiszaujvaros ITU World Cup on Aug. 9 • Won the Geneva ETU Triathlon European Cup on July 20, besting the field by more than 2 minutes • Finished 14th at ITU World Triathlon Hamburg • Earned third at the USA Triathlon Elite National Championships as the third American at ITU World Triathlon Chicago, finishing 21st overall • Cracked the top 10 with an eighth-place finish at the PATCO Triathlon Pan American Championships in Dallas, Texas, on May 31 • 33rd at ITU World Triathlon Yokohama and 38th at ITU World Triathlon Cape Town

Quick Facts
Parents are Ruth and Joe • Swam for the University of Maryland • Was an IRONMAN 70.3 World Champion as an age-group triathlete in 2008 • Completed a few triathlons as a child, but did her first sprint triathlon as an adult in 2006 • Favorite triathlon is Escape from Alcatraz • Proudest moment in triathlon was her 14th place at WTS Hamburg in 2014 because it was a breakthrough moment that showed her she could compete at the highest level in ITU • Favorite workout is doing long runs • One of her hobbies is scouring Yelp photos for good restaurants • Favorite movie is Elizabeth • Favorite band is They Might be Giants • Enjoys making eggs and veggies in a tortilla but says a cheeseburger is her favorite post-race meal • Favorite all-time Olympic moment is the sprint for gold in the women’s triathlon in 2012 • Her biggest role model is her dad
